context: During China's 'opening up' period after the Cultural Revolution, mainland audiences were hungry for Cantopop and Mandopop from Hong Kong and Taiwan. Teresa Teng's songs, though officially banned on state radio, circulated widely on bootleg cassettes. Lo Ta-yu's 'Tomorrow Will Be Better' (inspired by 'We Are the World') became a pan-Chinese anthem.

tracks.title: Mingri Hui Genghao (Tomorrow Will Be Better)
Ai Zai Shenqiu (Love in Deep Autumn)
Yue Liang Dai Biao Wo De Xin (The Moon Represents My Heart)
Wo De Zhongguo Xin (My Chinese Heart)
Zhi Wei Ni Zhongqing (For Your Love Only)
Long De Chuanren (Descendants of the Dragon)
